subject: Identify verification commands
description: Determine what commands prove the completion claim. Match the claim type to its required evidence: tests pass → test runner, build succeeds → build command, bug fixed → reproducing test, linter clean → linter command, agent task complete → VCS diff. If no command can prove the claim, the claim cannot be made.

subject: Run the verification commands
description: Execute the full verification commands identified in the previous step. Run it fresh — not cached, not partial. Do not rely on previous runs or assume results. The command must execute completely: no early termination, no partial checks, no spot tests.

subject: Read the complete output
description: Check the full output of the verification commands. Examine: exit code, failure counts, error messages, warning counts. Do not skim or skip sections. If output is truncated, re-run with full output. Note the specific evidence: exact pass/fail counts, exact exit code, exact error text.

subject: Verify the claim against evidence
description: Compare the output evidence to the original claim. If evidence confirms: state the claim WITH specific evidence (counts, exit code, output excerpt). If evidence contradicts: state the ACTUAL status with evidence. Skip any step = invalid claim.
